rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant Gibbs Cox, Inc.¿S Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 17, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant Gibbs Cox, Inc.¿s memorandum of costs is denied. The motion was filed after the deadline set by the parties. Although it is unfortunate that the parties¿ commendable meet and confer efforts resulted in a situation where the plaintiffs¿ motion is denied as untimely, on the current record the court lacks authority to relieve plaintiffs of the untimeliness. The hearing will be at 10 AM, not 9:30 AM. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD REENTS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 17, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant Cooper Industries, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages is granted. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Reents was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Reents¿ deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Further, purported undisputed material fact 16 in defendant¿s separate statement states that plaintiff¿s interrogatory responses failed to identify any facts, witnesses or documents supporting their claims that Ronald Johnson (sic) was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Among the evidence cited to support this purported undisputed fact are plaintiffs¿ response to special interrogatories nos. 16 and 18, submitted with the moving papers. Plaintiffs¿ responses to nos. 16 and 18 show that the purported undisputed fact 16 is not correct. In those responses, plaintiffs identified, although generally, persons with knowledge and documents supporting plaintiffs¿ contentions. Defendant¿s separate statement makes no mention of that identification. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. At the conclusion of the hearing on defendant¿s motion for summary judgment, the court will hear defendant¿s ex parte application that was deferred from the ex parte calendar on March 8. The hearing will be at 10 AM, not 9:30 AM. = (220/HEK)
